expectations.text: backup sourceм—җ нҷҲ л””л үнҶ лҰ¬(/home лҳҗлҠ” /home/user л“ұ)к°Җ нҸ¬н•ЁлҗЁ

expectations.evidence: profiles.toml line 11: `exclude = ["**/.cache", "**/node_modules"]` вҖ” `**/.cache` pattern excludes .cache directories at any depth

expectations.text: node_modules кҙҖл Ё exclude нҢЁн„ҙмқҙ нҸ¬н•ЁлҗЁ
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml line 11: `exclude = ["**/.cache", "**/node_modules"]` вҖ” `**/node_modules` pattern excludes node_modules directories at any depth

expectations.text: мғҲлІҪ 2мӢң schedule м„Өм •мқҙ нҸ¬н•ЁлҗЁ (02:00 лҳҗлҠ” мң мӮ¬ н‘ңнҳ„)
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml line 12: `schedule = "*-*-* 02:00:00"` вҖ” systemd OnCalendar expression for daily 2 AM

expectations.text: м¶ңл Ҙмқҙ мҳ¬л°”лҘё TOML кө¬мЎ°мқҙл©° profiles.toml нҢҢмқјлЎң м ҖмһҘлҗЁ
expectations.evidence: File `profiles.toml` exists in the outputs directory and contains syntactically valid TOML with properly nested sections: [global], ["home-backup"], [home-backup.backup], [home-backup.retention]

claims.claim: exclude patterns use ** glob to match at any depth
claims.evidence: profiles.toml uses `**/.cache` and `**/node_modules` which are standard restic glob patterns matching nested directories
This is a valid resticprofile option documented in explanation.md that registers the schedule under systemd as a system-level timer

claims.claim: `initialize = true` auto-runs restic init if needed
claims.evidence: explanation.md correctly states this option triggers `restic init` on first run вҖ” this is standard resticprofile behavior

explanation.md note at bottom: 'after-backupмқҖ forgetл§Ң мӢӨн–үн•ҳл©°, pruneмқҖ н•„мҡ” мӢң лі„лҸ„лЎң мҳҲм•Ҫн•ҳкұ°лӮҳ мҲҳлҸҷ мӢӨн–үн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.' This is accurate вҖ” resticprofile's after-backup runs forget without --prune by default unless prune is explicitly set

suggestions.assertion: м¶ңл Ҙмқҙ мҳ¬л°”лҘё TOML кө¬мЎ°мқҙл©° profiles.toml нҢҢмқјлЎң м ҖмһҘлҗЁ
suggestions.reason: This assertion passes if any syntactically valid TOML file named profiles.toml exists, even one with completely wrong content. Consider splitting into two separate assertions: one for filename and one that checks at least one key structural element (e.g., a named profile section exists, or the TOML can be parsed without error).

No assertion checks that `prune` behavior is configured вҖ” the user asked to 'лӮҳлЁём§ҖлҠ” м •лҰ¬н•ҙмӨҳ' (clean up the rest). The generated config uses `after-backup = true` which only runs `forget`, not `prune`. Old snapshot metadata is removed but actual data blocks are not freed until `restic prune` runs separately. An assertion checking for either `prune = true` in the retention section or a note explaining this limitation would make the eval more discriminating.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass and the output is genuinely correct. However, the eval misses a meaningful gap: the user's request to clean up old backups is only half-addressed вҖ” forget runs but prune does not, meaning disk space isn't actually reclaimed. The explanation.md does flag this correctly, but no assertion catches it.
